steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: A comma precedes the blank, so we need a word that avoids a comma splice. If the blank creates a second finite verb (and thus a second independent clause), the comma becomes a comma splice. A participle instead creates a participial phrase, which is not an independent clause and can follow a comma correctly.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'range': finite verb — creates a second independent clause after the comma, producing a comma splice. ✗
  • Choice B — 'can range': finite verb — same comma-splice problem. ✗
  • Choice C — 'ranging': present participle — creates a participial phrase, not an independent clause. Can correctly follow a comma. ✓
  • Choice D — 'will range': finite verb — same comma-splice problem. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
